prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a bep has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the bep-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a bep, a zop, and a zant. The bep is underneath the zop. The zop is to-the-left-of the zant. The bep has a purple button on top, a yellow button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The zop has a yellow button on top and a purple button on bottom. The zant has a blue button on top and a purple button on side. Pressing the purple button on the bep activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a snig, a ikk, and a mek. The ikk is underneath the snig. The snig is to-the-left-of the mek. The snig has a yellow button on top and a purple button on side. The ikk has a purple button on top, a yellow button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The mek has a blue button on top and a yellow button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the bep-analog, and which button activates it?
